question_text: Rural Development Initiative: refers to the use of technology to address the economic and social challenges caused by limited access to resources in rural areas. The following are examples of rural development initiatives. A. A local government partnered with a tech firm to provide free Wi-Fi in a rural town, allowing residents to access online resources and connect with the global community. B. A company developed a low-cost, user-friendly tablet with a long battery life, designed for rural areas with limited electricity. Many rural residents purchased the device. C. A top-ranked university in the capital city set up an online platform and remotely broadcast lectures to a rural university, sharing high-quality educational resources and increasing the number of rural students admitted to top universities. D. Li Ming, a young entrepreneur, started an e-commerce business selling handmade crafts from rural artisans, increasing their income and improving their quality of life.

options: A logistics company teamed up with an Internet giant to recruit a group of courier from poor households in remote mountainous areas. After professional training, they took up posts to help them embark on the path of getting rid of poverty and getting rich.
A mobile phone manufacturer for the rural market introduced a low-cost, easy-to-operate, smart phone with a large screen and a long standby time. Villagers have replaced their old mobile phones.

A key middle school in the provincial capital has set up an online classroom and remotely synchronized live broadcast to a middle school in a poor county, so that teachers and students in both places share high-quality teaching resources. In recent years, the number of students admitted to key universities in the county has increased significantly.

Xiao Zhou, who returned home after graduation, took a few classmates to open an online store and sold his own planted red dates online. Sales gradually increased and the days became more and more prosperous.
metadata.transformation_description: Changed context from poverty alleviation to rural development, and modified examples to fit the new context.

question_text: The synergy effect: refers to a holistic mechanism formed by the interaction of many factors, which can reduce the incompleteness or shortcomings of a problem to a minimum, or even disappear, and finally make this problem well resolved. Which of the following are not the synergy effect? A. In 2015, the number of South Korean mobile phone users exceeded 40 million. More and more people used mobile phones to fully obtain and disseminate information. Information and public opinion have also been more widely distributed in three dimensions. South Korea is forming a transparent information space and public opinion. B. In order to form an effective mechanism for a vigorous green ecological civilization and fully concentrate the wisdom of the people, a city has set up a civic supervision committee, a civic hearing, and an environmental protection organization, so that the general public can better advise and play a supervisory role. C. In order to prevent doctors from taking medical rebates, a province has set up a special patient complaint hotline for patients in various hospitals. D. In order to manage the private use of public buses, a local government takes photos of the suspected cars at any time and sends them to the relevant authorities.

options: In 2012, the number of Chinese Internet users exceeded 500 million. More and more people used Internet media to fully obtain and disseminate information. Information and public opinion have also been more widely distributed in three dimensions. China is forming a transparent information space and public opinion.

In order to form an effective mechanism for a vigorous green ecological civilization and fully concentrate the wisdom of the people, a city has set up a civic supervision committee, a civic hearing, and an environmental protection organization, so that the general public can better advise and play a supervisory role.
In order to prevent doctors from taking medical rebates, a province has set up a special patient complaint hotline for patients in various hospitals.
In order to manage the private use of public buses, a local government takes photos of the suspected cars at any time and sends them to.
metadata.transformation_description: Changed the name of the effect from 'Shadowless lamp effect' to 'Synergy effect', changed the country from China to South Korea, and changed the year from 2012 to 2015.

Child: Mom, why is there no cotton in marshmallows? Mother: This is normal! Did you hide a hero in the hero brand ink?
Customer: Little sister, why are there no pearls in the pearl milk tea you sell? Salesperson: What a curious thing! There are no couples in the couple's lungs!
metadata.transformation_description: Changed context to a diner and waiter, and objects to honey toast and chairlift.

question_text: Is Rachel a stepsister of Emily? Is Olivia related to Ava? In complex social networks, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of all one's acquaintances. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: First of all, no friend of Ethan is both a classmate of Liam and a friend of Noah. Next, whoever is not a classmate of Liam is a colleague of Olivia. Plus, being a colleague of Olivia is necessary for not being a friend of Noah. We may conclude: Being a friend of Ethan is sufficient for being a colleague of Olivia. Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid? A. valid B. invalid

metadata.transformation_description: Changed names (Siri to Rachel, Mary to Emily, Susan to Olivia, Kate to Ava, Daisy to Ethan, Margie to Liam, Dori to Noah), and context (large families to complex social networks, relatives to acquaintances, aunt to friend, half-sister to classmate, sister to colleague)

question_text: According to a review of 75 studies of patients suffering from severely debilitating post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), a large majority of the patients reported that engaging in creative activities immediately reduced their PTSD symptoms. Yet engaging in creative activities is not used to treat PTSD even though the conventional treatments, which use therapy and medication, often have serious side effects. Which of the following, if true, best explains the fact that engaging in creative activities is not used as a treatment for PTSD?
correct_answer: PTSD symptoms return in full force as soon as the patient stops engaging in creative activities for even a short period.

options: The dramatic shifts in mood connected with sleep and wakefulness have not been traced to particular changes in brain chemistry.
Keeping depressed patients awake is more difficult than keeping awake people who are not depressed.
Depression returns in full force as soon as the patient sleeps for even a few minutes.
For a small percentage of depressed patients, missing a night's sleep induces a temporary sense of euphoria.
metadata.transformation_description: Changed the condition from depression to PTSD, the treatment from sleep-deprivation to engaging in creative activities, and the number of studies from 61 to 75.

question_text: After graduating from medical school, doctors rarely use advanced astronomy or sociology, but they frequently encounter problems related to medical practice management. However, when astronomy and sociology are compulsory courses in medical school, medical practice management is only an elective lecture, and very few students take this lecture. Which of the following points are best supported by the above discussion?
correct_answer: If it is important to teach medical students about disciplines that they will face later in their professional lives, then medical practice management should be an important part of medical school.

options: Unless high school focuses more on non-professional subjects like family economics, people who graduate from high school will never easily make decisions in daily life.
Although family economics is the most useful discipline for people who have to make decisions in their daily lives, usually mathematics and history can also help people make these decisions.
Mathematics, history and other subjects that are not directly related to one's daily life should not be a required part of the high school curriculum.
If it is important to teach high school students about disciplines that they will face later in everyday life, then home economics should be an important part of the high school curriculum.
metadata.transformation_description: Changed context to medical school and medical profession, replaced family economics with medical practice management, and replaced high school with medical school.

question_text: A restaurant hired 35 new staff members this year.① Some of the newly hired staff members are from outside the city; ② Of the newly hired staff members, the most experienced workers are not from outside the city; ③ Some of the newly hired staff members are not from outside the city. If only one of the above three judgments is true, then the following items correctly represent the number of staff members from outside the city among the newly hired staff in the restaurant. A. Only 1 staff member is from outside the city. B. 0 staff members are from outside the city. C. 0 staff members are not from outside the city. D. Only 1 staff member is not from outside the city.

options: Only 1 person is a foreigner.
0 employees are foreigners.
0 employees are not foreigners.
Only 1 person is not a foreigner.
metadata.transformation_description: Changed context from 'unit' to 'restaurant', 'employees' to 'staff members', and 'academic qualifications' to 'most experienced workers'.

question_text: After completing their degree, software developers rarely use advanced physics or philosophy, but they frequently encounter problems related to project management. However, when physics and philosophy are compulsory courses in the undergraduate curriculum, project management is only an elective module, and very few students take this module. Which of the following points are best supported by the above discussion?
correct_answer: If it is important to teach undergraduate students about disciplines that they will face later in their professional lives, then project management should be an important part of the undergraduate curriculum.

options: Unless high school focuses more on non-professional subjects like family economics, people who graduate from high school will never easily make decisions in daily life.
Although family economics is the most useful discipline for people who have to make decisions in their daily lives, usually mathematics and history can also help people make these decisions.
Mathematics, history and other subjects that are not directly related to one's daily life should not be a required part of the high school curriculum.
If it is important to teach high school students about disciplines that they will face later in everyday life, then home economics should be an important part of the high school curriculum.
metadata.transformation_description: Changed context to software development and undergraduate education, replaced family economics with project management, and replaced high school with undergraduate curriculum.

question_text: Is Olivia a fan of the University of Michigan? Are supporters of the University of Ohio State devotees of the University of California, Berkeley? In the world of college athletics, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of the mutual admiration and dislike. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: To begin with, whoever is an ex-fan of the University of Texas and a friend of the University of Georgia is not an ex-fan of the University of Florida. Therefore, every ex-fan of the University of Florida is not an ex-fan of the University of Texas or not a friend of the University of Georgia.

metadata.transformation_description: Changed the context from European football to college athletics, replaced team names with university names, and changed the character's name from Fred to Olivia.

question_text: Here comes a perfectly valid argument: First premise: Every friend of Olivia is a friend of Ava. Second premise: Some friend of Ava is not a neighbor of Jackson. We may conclude that not every neighbor of Jackson is a friend of Olivia. Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

metadata.transformation_description: Changed names and context from classmates to friends and schoolmates to neighbors.

question_text: The unity mechanism refers to a holistic mechanism formed by the interaction of many factors, which can reduce the incompleteness or shortcomings of a problem to a minimum, or even disappear, and finally make this problem well resolved. Which of the following are not the unity mechanism? A. In 2016, the number of Russian mobile phone users exceeded 150 million. More and more people used mobile phones to fully obtain and disseminate information. Information and public opinion have also been more widely distributed in three dimensions. Russia is forming a transparent information space and public opinion. B. In order to form an effective mechanism for a vigorous green ecological civilization and fully concentrate the wisdom of the people, a city has set up a civic supervision committee, a civic hearing, and an environmental protection organization, so that the general public can better advise and play a supervisory role. C. In order to prevent government officials from taking kickbacks, a government agency has set up a special citizen complaint hotline for citizens in various districts. D. In order to manage the private use of public recreational facilities, a local government takes photos of the suspected areas at any time and sends them to the relevant authorities.

options: In 2012, the number of Chinese Internet users exceeded 500 million. More and more people used Internet media to fully obtain and disseminate information. Information and public opinion have also been more widely distributed in three dimensions. China is forming a transparent information space and public opinion.

In order to form an effective mechanism for a vigorous green ecological civilization and fully concentrate the wisdom of the people, a city has set up a civic supervision committee, a civic hearing, and an environmental protection organization, so that the general public can better advise and play a supervisory role.
In order to prevent doctors from taking medical rebates, a province has set up a special patient complaint hotline for patients in various hospitals.
In order to manage the private use of public buses, a local government takes photos of the suspected cars at any time and sends them to.
metadata.transformation_description: Changed the concept name to 'unity mechanism', changed the country and object in option A to Russia and mobile phone users, changed the context in option C to a government agency and government officials, and changed the object in option D to public recreational facilities.

question_text: Although I've never met you, at first glance, you know that you are a firefighter who is also a paramedic. It's very simple, because you have the bravery of a firefighter, but you also have the medical skills of a paramedic. Which of the following prerequisites should be added to the above reasoning?

options: To become a military doctor, you must have both the temperament of a doctor and a soldier.
Military doctors are more military than ordinary doctors.
As long as he is a military doctor, he will definitely show his doctor's grace and military personality.
Everyone who has the demeanor of a doctor and the spirit of a soldier must be a military doctor, regardless of his medical skills.
metadata.transformation_description: Changed occupation from military doctor to firefighter who is also a paramedic, and traits from demeanor of a doctor and spirit of a soldier to bravery of a firefighter and medical skills of a paramedic.
